### Task: add-territorial-scoring
- **Description**: GDD §1 reference doc mentions "territorial tiles(×20)" as a Perfection scoring category. Currently `ScoreCalculator.computeTribeScore()` does not include territorial tiles. Each city claims tiles within its territory radius (GDD §5.4: radius = city level). Add 20 points per territorial tile to the score. Territory tiles = all tiles within `city.level` hex distance of the city that are not captured by another city. Count unique tiles (no double-counting overlapping cities).
- **Location**: `src/entities/ScoreCalculator.ts` — add territorial tile count
- **Success criteria**:
  - `computeTribeScore()` includes +20 per territorial tile
  - Territory tiles calculated as tiles within `city.level` hex distance
  - No double-counting for overlapping city territories
  - 1+ new test verifying territorial score calculation
  - `npx vitest run` — all tests pass
- **Coach checks**:
  - Read ScoreCalculator.ts — verify territorial tile logic
  - Read test — verify correct scoring for known configurations
  - Run all tests

### Task: fix-persistent-pause-game-overlay
- **Description**: The game has no pause/resume functionality. When playing, there's no way to pause the game. Add an Escape key handler that shows a semi-transparent overlay with "PAUSED" text and a "Resume (ESC)" button. The overlay should stop the game timer (if any) and dim the game canvas beneath. Pressing Escape again or clicking Resume should close the overlay. The pause state should only be active during the human player's turn (not during AI processing, which should keep running).
- **Location**: `src/scenes/GameScene.ts` — pause/resume handler, new UI group
- **Success criteria**:
  - Pressing Escape during human turn shows a centered "PAUSED" overlay with semi-transparent backdrop

  - No interaction with game elements while paused (events blocked)

  - `npx vitest run` — all existing tests (481+) pass
  - 2+ new tests verifying pause/resume state management

## Next Batch — Tech & City Menu Fixes (2026-06-23)

> These tasks address live-site bugs reported at hex.codeovertcp.com.

### Task: fix-tech-panel-text-overlap
- **Description**: The tech panel (toggle via `[ TECH ]` button) renders 10 tech series columns in a 680px-wide panel. The column width formula `Math.min(220, Math.floor(600 / seriesKeys.length))` yields only **60px per column** with 10 series. Tech names like "Organization" (96px), "Frostwork" (72px), "Aquaculture" (88px) at 14px monospace overflow into the neighbouring column. Fix so each column's text doesn't overlap adjacent columns — either by making the panel scrollable, splitting into multiple rows, using a narrower condensed layout (icons + short labels), or reducing columns shown based on human tribe's unlocked series.
- **Location**: `GameScene.ts` lines 1262-1320 (`showTechPanel()`)
- **Success criteria**:
  - All visible tech names and descriptions render within their column bounds with no horizontal overlap
  - When the human tribe has researched no techs, the panel is still readable (no clipped labels)
  - Tribe-specific series (Polaris frostwork, Cymanti fungiculture, Elyrion ecology) don't waste space for non-matching tribes
  - `npx vitest run` — all existing tests pass
- **Coach checks**:
  - Navigate to hex.codeovertcp.com, start a game, click `[ TECH ]` — verify no text overlapping in any column
  - Play as a tribe from at least 2 different families (e.g. Xin-xi + Elyrion) — verify panel renders readably

### Task: fix-city-menu-stays-on-screen-after-scroll
- **Description**: The city menu (shown when clicking an own city) is positioned using `sx = p.x + scrollX + 40; sy = p.y + scrollY - 20` but the text objects **do not call `setScrollFactor(0)`**. This means the position bakes in the camera scroll offset at creation time but the text also inherits scrollFactor=1 (default), causing the menu to appear offset from its intended position when the camera moves. Fix the menu so it appears at a stable offset from the city hex and stays attached even after camera scrolling.
- **Location**: `GameScene.ts` lines 1341-1343 (position calc) and line 1646 (text creation in `showCityMenu()`)
- **Success criteria**:
  - Clicking an own city shows a menu of trainable units, upgrades, buildings, etc.
  - Scrolling the camera after the menu is open keeps the menu attached to the correct position relative to the city
  - The menu doesn't overlap the top HUD bar
  - Menu closes when clicking elsewhere on the map
  - `npx vitest run` — all existing tests pass
- **Coach checks**:
  - Start a game, end turn until the capital city is controllable, click the city
  - Scroll the map (drag) — verify menu follows the city on screen
  - Click elsewhere — verify menu closes
  - Check E2E test at `tests-e2e/gameplay.spec.ts:188` (`city tile opens and closes the city menu`) still works

## Next Batch — Generated by Coach (2026-06-24)

> These tasks were auto-generated by the Coach after backlog health check: 0 remaining tasks, stalled loop detected. Code review found 2 real scoring bugs.

### Task: fix-grand-bazaar-score
- **Description**: `GameScene.calcScore()` does NOT include the Grand Bazaar +400 score. `TradeRouteSystem.applyConnectionBonuses()` calculates and returns the score but `GameScene` ignores the return value (line 1574), and `calcScore()` (lines 918-926) never checks `city.hasGrandBazaar`. This means Perfection mode final scores are incorrect for any player who builds 5+ city connections.
- **Location**: `src/scenes/GameScene.ts` lines 918-926 (`calcScore()`)
- **Fix**: Add `const grandBazaarScore = tribe.cities.reduce((sum, c) => sum + (c.hasGrandBazaar ? 400 : 0), 0);` to `calcScore()` and include in the return sum.
- **Success criteria**:
  - `calcScore()` returns correct score including +400 for cities with `hasGrandBazaar === true`
  - When no city has Grand Bazaar, score is unchanged
  - `npx vitest run` — all existing tests pass
  - 1+ new test verifying Grand Bazaar score inclusion
- **Coach checks**:
  - Read `calcScore()` — verify `hasGrandBazaar` checked
  - Verify in test: city with GrandBazaar contributes +400, without contributes 0
  - Run all tests — all pass

### Task: fix-level-score-formula
- **Description**: GDD §1.2 score formula says "City Level >1: 50 points per level above 1 (L3 = 100 pts)". But `calcScore()` line 922 does `c.level * 20` which gives L2=40, L3=60, L4=80, L5=100. Should be `(c.level - 1) * 50`: L1=0, L2=50, L3=100, L4=150, L5=200.
- **Location**: `src/scenes/GameScene.ts` line 922
- **Fix**: Change `c.level * 20` to `(c.level - 1) * 50`
- **Success criteria**:
  - L1 city contributes 0 level score
  - L2 contributes 50, L3=100, L4=150, L5=200
  - Existing tests still pass
  - 1+ new test verifying level score formula
- **Coach checks**:
  - Read `calcScore()` — verify formula change matches GDD
  - Test: L1=0, L2=50, L3=100, L4=150, L5=200
  - Run all tests — all pass

### Task: gdd-9-fix-park-scoring-status
- **Description**: **ALREADY COMPLETED** (commit 6993877). Changed GDD.md Park scoring from `[ ]` to `[x]`. Keep as placeholder — will be removed next cycle.
- **Success criteria**: ✅ Done

## Next Batch — Generated by Coach (2026-06-24 07:10)

> These tasks were auto-generated after backlog health check: 0 remaining tasks. Investigation found 3 pre-existing issues for the next dev cycle.

### Task: fix-e2e-playwright-config-collision
- **Description**: `pw-e2e.config.ts` has `testDir: './tests'` but the `tests/` directory contains 12 vitest files with `.test.ts` extensions. Playwright's default `testMatch` pattern (`**/*.@(spec|test).?(c|m)[jt]s?(x)`) picks up these vitest files, causing collisions when `npm run test:e2e` is executed. Fix: either add `testMatch: '**/*.spec.ts'` to the Playwright config, or rename to `tests-e2e/` and create at least one smoke-test spec for the core game loop (tribe selection → start game → end turn).
- **Location**: `pw-e2e.config.ts`
- **Fix**: Add `testMatch: ['**/*.spec.ts']` or rename directory to `tests-e2e/` and update `testDir`.
- **Success criteria**:
  - `npm run test:e2e` runs only intended e2e tests (not vitest unit tests)
  - At least 1 basic smoke test: navigate to `http://localhost:3001`, verify `<canvas>` element exists
  - `npm run test` (vitest) still passes all 452 tests
- **Coach checks**:
  - Run `npm run test:e2e` — verify only Playwright tests execute
  - Verify vitest tests are not picked up by Playwright

### Task: add-vite-bundle-code-splitting
- **Description**: The production build emits a single 1.5MB JS bundle (`dist/assets/index-*.js`, 362KB gzipped). Vite warns: "Some chunks are larger than 500 kB after minification." Split Phaser (which constitutes most of the bundle) into a separate vendor chunk via `build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks` in `vite.config.ts`. This improves initial load time via browser parallel downloads and better caching (Phaser changes rarely, game code changes often).
- **Location**: `vite.config.ts`
- **Fix**: Add `build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks: { phaser: ['phaser'] }` to the Vite config.
- **Success criteria**:
  - `npm run build` no longer emits the large-chunk warning
  - Output has at least 2 JS bundles: `phaser-*.js` and `index-*.js`
  - `npx vitest run` — all tests still pass
- **Coach checks**:
  - Run `npm run build` — verify no chunk-size warning
  - Check `dist/assets/` — verify phaser is split into separate chunk
  - Verify game loads correctly on live site

### Task: add-sound-toggle-ui
- **Description**: The game has no audio controls visible in the HUD. When Phaser audio is enabled (via WebAudio or HTML5 Audio), a mute/unmute button should exist for accessibility and user preference. Add a small speaker icon button to the top HUD bar that toggles `game.sound.mute`. If the game does not load any audio assets yet, this can be wired as a no-op UI stub ready for when audio assets are added.
- **Location**: `src/scenes/GameScene.ts` (HUD render section)
- **Fix**: Add a mute toggle button (🔇/🔊) to the top-right HUD area that calls `this.game.sound.mute = !this.game.sound.mute` and updates the icon. Persist preference in `localStorage`.
- **Success criteria**:
  - Mute button visible in-game HUD
  - Clicking toggles icon between 🔊 and 🔇
  - Mute state persists across page reload (localStorage)
  - `npx vitest run` — all existing tests pass
- **Coach checks**:
  - Start a game — verify mute button appears in HUD
  - Click it — verify icon changes state
  - Reload page — verify mute state is restored

### Task: fix-mute-button-overlap
- **Description**: The mute toggle button (`this.muteBtn`) at position (740, 10) overlaps with the end turn button at (660, 10) with `fontSize: '20px'`. The end turn text "[ END TURN ]" at 20px monospace spans ~660-780 while the mute emoji at (740, 10) spans ~740-762, creating a ~22px overlap in the hit areas. Fix by either: (a) moving muteBtn further right, (b) placing it on the left side, or (c) adjusting the HUD layout to properly space all buttons.
- **Location**: `src/scenes/GameScene.ts` lines 256 (end turn), 280 (muteBtn)
- **Success criteria**:
  - No visual or clickable overlap between mute button and end turn button
  - Both buttons are fully clickable without interference
  - Mute button stays visible and accessible
  - `npx vitest run` — all existing tests pass
- **Coach checks**:
  - Verify in browser that clicking the end turn button doesn't accidentally toggle mute
  - Run tests — all pass

### Task: fix-deploy-systemd-execstartpre
- **Description**: The systemd service (`polytopia-clone.service`) has no `ExecStartPre` to clean up stale processes on port 3001 before starting. PID 2122063 (`serve dist -p 3001 --cors`) from 07:17 persists across service restarts because systemd only kills its own child processes. Add `ExecStartPre=/usr/bin/env sh -c 'lsof -ti:3001 | xargs -r kill -KILL'` to the service file so stale processes are killed before the new instance starts.
- **Location**: `~/.config/systemd/user/polytopia-clone.service`
- **Success criteria**:
  - `systemctl --user restart polytopia-clone` binds to port 3001 (not 3002)
  - Stale `serve` processes on port 3001 are killed before the new instance starts
  - `systemctl --user status polytopia-clone` shows listening on :3001
  - `curl http://localhost:3001/` returns 200
  - All 452 tests pass
- **Coach checks**:
  - Run `systemctl --user daemon-reload && systemctl --user restart polytopia-clone`
  - Verify `ss -tlnp | grep \":3001\"` shows only 1 node process
  - Verify tunnel still serves HTTP 200
  - Run all tests

### Task: fix-mobile-game-scaling
- **Description**: The game canvas is hardcoded to 800x600 pixels. On mobile browsers, small viewports (<800px wide), or when the browser is resized, the canvas overflows or gets cropped with no responsive scaling. Phaser's Scale Manager supports FIT mode which auto-resizes the canvas while maintaining aspect ratio. Currently the `#game` div has no scale constraints and the Phaser game config doesn't set a Scale Manager mode. Fix: configure Phaser Scale Manager with `mode: Phaser.Scale.FIT` and `autoCenter: Phaser.Scale.CENTER_BOTH`, and ensure the CSS body/game container allows scaling (max-width: 100vw, max-height: 100vh).
- **Location**: `src/main.ts` (or wherever the Phaser Game config is created), CSS in `index.html`
- **Success criteria**:
  - Game canvas scales to fit browser viewport on mobile (<800px wide)
  - Game maintains 4:3 aspect ratio at all sizes
  - No visual cropping or overflow on narrow screens
  - Desktop at ≥800px wide shows game at 800x600 (unchanged)
  - `npx vitest run` — all existing tests pass
- **Coach checks**:
  - Resize browser to <800px wide — verify canvas scales down proportionally
  - Open on a mobile-sized viewport (375x667) — verify game fits without scrollbars
  - Verify desktop still shows 800x600 centered
  - Run all tests

### Task: fix-ai-difficulty-levels
- **Description**: The tribe selection screen has map type and game mode options but no difficulty selection. All AI tribes use the same behavior parameters (expansion rate, attack frequency, tech priority). Add Easy/Medium/Hard difficulty options to the tribe selection screen that scale AI behavior: Easy = AI expands slowly, attacks rarely, researches defensively; Medium = current behavior (balanced); Hard = AI expands aggressively, attacks frequently, prioritizes military techs. Difficulty selection appears as a third row below Map and Mode.
- **Location**: `src/scenes/TribeSelectScene.ts` (selection screen), AI decision logic files
- **Success criteria**:
  - Tribe selection screen shows "Difficulty: Easy / Medium / Hard" toggle row
  - Medium matches current AI behavior exactly (no regression)
  - Easy AI: slower expansion, fewer attacks, lower tech priority
  - Hard AI: faster expansion, more frequent attacks, military tech priority
  - 3+ new tests verifying difficulty affects AI decisions
  - `npx vitest run` — all tests pass
- **Coach checks**:
  - Select Easy, start game — verify AI is less aggressive than Medium
  - Select Hard, start game — verify AI is more aggressive than Medium
  - Verify Medium matches previous behavior (no regression)
  - Run all tests

### Task: fix-game-speed-control
- **Description**: The game has no speed selection. In real Polytopia, game speed affects tech costs and production: Normal (1.0x), Fast (0.75x), Blitz (0.5x). Add a speed selection row to the tribe selection screen (between Mode and Difficulty). When speed is selected, multiply tech research costs and unit/building production costs by the speed multiplier. Speed selection persists for the game session.
- **Location**: `src/scenes/TribeSelectScene.ts` (UI), `src/objects/TechTree.ts` (tech costs), `src/objects/City.ts` (production costs)
- **Success criteria**:
  - Tribe selection screen shows "Speed: Normal / Fast / Blitz" toggle row
  - Normal speed matches current costs exactly (no regression)
  - Fast speed reduces tech/production costs to 75%
  - Blitz speed reduces tech/production costs to 50%
  - Speed selection is stored and used throughout the game
  - 3+ new tests verifying speed affects costs
  - `npx vitest run` — all existing tests pass

  - Start a Normal game — verify costs match previous behavior (no regression)
